mechanism: Population of subjects receives stimulus vignettes varying in moral valence of side-effect; responses to knowledge attribution questions collected on Likert scale; asymmetry detected via univariate statistics testing for differences between conditions based on moral status of outcome.

Experimental manipulation of cultural distance in moral judgment scenarios; participants rate agreement with objectivity claims on 7-point scale in between-subjects and within-subjects designs; pattern detected via mixed-model ANOVA revealing main effect of cultural condition and maintained effect in within-subjects design.

Experimental presentation of knowledge ascription scenarios with manipulated contrast contexts via explicit wording, knowledge-wh constructions, or conversational setup; participant agreement ratings on 7-point scale; univariate tests reveal contrast sensitivity across three independent manipulations.

Experimental presentation of determinism descriptions (manipulating bypassing and modal scope) to participants; collection of Likert-scale responses to moral responsibility and bypassing judgments; mediation analysis testing whether bypassing mediates description effects on moral responsibility via multiple regression.

Measurement of inhibitory control via retrieval-induced inhibition task (median RT ratios); measurement of L2 phonological perception via ABX categorization error rates; measurement of L2 production via categorical and acoustic analysis; partial correlations of inhibition scores with perception/production accuracy controlling for vocabulary proficiency.

Experimental vignettes manipulating stakes or salience in knowledge ascription scenarios; participant ratings on agreement scales; comparison of first-wave results (Feltz, May, Buckwalter) showing absence of stakes effect via ANOVA; second-wave reanalysis of Pinillos and Sripada-Stanley data isolating modal vs. knowledge effects via subsidiary probes (belief, guess, hope).
